gauge.name: Cumberland River near Hermitage

impacts.statement: Widespread significant flooding continues to impact areas adjacent to the river, homes in Neelys Bend and E.N. Peeler Park, the Hampton Park subdivision, the Hermitage Ridge subdivision, the Hopewell neighborhood, and other homes and properties.

Widespread significant flooding along the Cumberland River from the Hermitage Golf Course to the River Trace subdivision is impacting Golf Courses adjacent to the river, homes in Neelys Bend and E.N. Peeler Park, the Hampton Park subdivision, the Hermitage Ridge subdivision, homes in the Hopewell neighborhood, and homes on Kingsway Dr, Gaile Dr, Charlie Gann Dr, and Heartland Dr.

Significant flooding is impacting golf courses adjacent to the river, homes in Neelys Bend and E.N. Peeler Park, the Hampton Park subdivision, the Hermitage Ridge subdivision, homes on Kingsway Dr, Gaile Dr, Charlie Gann Dr, and Heartland Dr. Water begins to approach homes on Riner Dr, and on Hermitage St and Commerce St in the Hopewell neighborhood.

Flooding continues to impact locations adjacent to the river, including golf courses, homes in Neelys Bend on Hidden Acres Dr, Menees Ln, Neelys Bend Rd, E.N. Peeler Park, homes in the Hampton Park subdivision and the Hermitage Ridge subdivision near the river, and homes on Kingsway Dr, Gaile Dr, Charlie Gann Dr, and Heartland Dr. Center St and Capital St begin to flood and may be impassable at higher levels.

Widespread flooding continues along the river, including golf courses adjacent to the river, properties in Neelys Bend on Hidden Acres Dr, Menees Ln, Neeleys Bend Rd, and E.N. Peeler Park. Also impacted are properties near the river in the Hampton Park subdivision, the Hermitage Ridge subdivision, on Kingsway Dr, and on Heartland Dr. Water continues to encroach on the K.R. Harrington water facility, and access may be limited at higher levels as portions of Stones River Rd are inundated.

Many areas along the Cumberland River are flooding, including golf courses adjacent to the river, properties in Neelys Bend on Hidden Acres Dr, Menees Ln, Neelys Bend Rd, and E.N. Peeler Park. Water impacts properties in Hampton Park on Aaronwood Ct and Granwood Blvd, in Hermitage Ridge on Hermitage Ridge Dr and Linden Green Dr, and on Kingsway Dr. Heartland Dr begins to flood and may be impassable at higher stages, and water begins to impact structures at the K.R. Harrington water facility.

Flooding continues along the Cumberland River, covering most of the Hermitage Golf Course and Gaylord Springs Golf Course. Properties along the river in Neelys Bend, including E.N. Peeler Park, are flooding and access may be limited at higher river levels. Water approaches properties in the Hampton Park subdivision on Aaronwood Ct and Granwood Blvd. Water approaches properties on Linden Green Dr and at the end of Hidden Acres Dr.

Flooding is occurring along the Cumberland River from the Hermitage Golf Course to near the River Trace subdivision. Water covers most of the Hermitage Golf Course and the Gaylord Springs Golf Course, and begins to impact properties along the river in Neelys Bend. Water begins to inundate property around the K.R. Harrington water facility.

Water is inundating portions of the Hermitage Golf Course and the Gaylord Springs Golf Course, and will significantly cover the remainder at higher levels. Areas along the Stones River, including the Stones River Greenway and the Heartland Park athletic fields, are also inundated.

Areas along the river continue to be inundated, including portions of the Hermitage Golf Course and the Gaylord Springs Golf Course. Water is also inundating areas along the Stones River, including portions of the Stones River Greenway and the Heartland Park athletic fields.

Low lying areas along the Cumberland River from the Hermitage Golf Course to near River Trace begin to be inundated. Water is also backing up on the Stones River and inundating adjacent low lying areas including portions of the Stones River Greenway.

dataAttribution.text: USGS--Water Resources of the United States
dataAttribution.title: US Geological Survey
Observations courtesy of U.S. Geological Survey

value.label: North American Vertical Datum of 1988
value.description: A fixed reference for elevations determined by geodetic leveling. The datum was derived from a general adjustment of the first-order terrestrial leveling nets of the United States, Canada, and Mexico. In the adjustment, only the height of the primary tidal bench mark, referenced to the International Great Lakes Datum of 1985 (IGLD 85) local mean sea level height value, at Father Point, Rimouski, Quebec, Canada was held fixed, thus providing minimum constraint. NAVD 88 and IGLD 85 are identical. However, NAVD 88 bench mark values are given in Helmert orthometric height units while IGLD 85 values are in dynamic heights. See International Great Lakes Datum of 1985, National Geodetic Vertical Datum of 1929, and geopotential difference. NAVD 88 should not be used as Mean Sea Level.

gauge.forecastReliability: Forecasts are not available. Only observed stages are available for this point.
